shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Processor must inform Controller of a legal requirement before processing when processing is required by Union/Member State law, unless public-interest law prohibits informing; this matches the regulation's processor-to-controller notification duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered when the processor is subject to a legal requirement to process, and both include the exception where law prohibits informing on public-interest grounds.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall inform' is mandatory and matches the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: timing 'before Processing' matches 'before processing'; recipient and object align.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: Processor informs Controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors; same obligation as regulation.
results.condition_check: Same triggering condition: in case of general written authorization.
results.modality_check: Shall inform; equal force to regulatory shall.
results.other_constraints_check: Same object and timing; contract adds controller objection mechanics and 30-day deemed acceptance, which do not weaken the notice duty.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both require the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract uses the same triggering condition, 'only on documented instructions from the Controller,' and even states the agreement itself constitutes those instructions.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: 'shall ... only' is mandatory and restrictive, meeting the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the object, actor, and recipient match; the contract is at least as strict as the regulation and adds no weakening constraint.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both clauses impose the processor's duty to allow for and contribute to audits/inspections conducted for the controller's oversight, with the contract expressly covering audits including inspections by an external qualified auditor mandated by the Controller.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract limits audits to those conducted during normal business hours and reasonably necessary, which is a reasonable narrowing/qualification and does not contradict the regulation's audit condition.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses mandatory language ('to allow for and contribute'), matching the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract is at least as specific and not weaker on manner/target: it covers audits including inspections, by an auditor mandated by the Controller, and adds confidentiality and purpose constraints that are stricter rather than looser.
results.contract_source_text: to allow for and contribute during normal business hours to reasonably necessary audits including inspections, conducted by an external qualified auditor mandated by the Controller, solely for the purpose of fulfilment of the Controller's obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR and for accurately stipulated research questions in this connection provided that such external qualified auditor is subject to and bound by confidentiality obligations as stipulated in clause 12 of this Agreement;

results.core_alignment_check: Same actor (processor), same obligation (ensure authorized persons processing personal data are bound by confidentiality), same object/recipient.
results.condition_check: No additional triggering condition beyond persons authorized to process personal data; contract matches.
results.modality_check: Ensures/shall ensure are equivalent mandatory obligations.
results.other_constraints_check: Same substantive confidentiality requirement; no weaker timing, scope, or location constraints.
results.contract_source_text: Processor ensures that persons authorised to process the Personal Data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality.

results.core_alignment_check: Same actor and duty: the processor remains liable to the controller for performance of sub-processor obligations; substance matches the regulation.
results.condition_check: No additional conditions in either text; compatible.
results.modality_check: Shall remain liable matches the mandatory regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Same target and scope: liability to the controller for sub-processor obligations; 'its' sub-processor is equivalent.
results.contract_source_text: Processor shall remain liable to the Controller for the performance of its Sub- processor's obligations.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both clauses impose on the processor a duty to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for security/protection of personal data and data subjects' rights/freedoms.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation ties measures to risk level; the contract requires measures sufficient to meet GDPR and protect rights, which is at least as protective and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Agrees to implement' is an обязательство equivalent to 'shall implement'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's purpose of ensuring GDPR compliance and protection of data subjects' rights is substantively aligned with ensuring security appropriate to risk for rights and freedoms.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment And Prior Consultation
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both provisions impose a processor obligation to assist the controller with GDPR DPIA-related compliance, including prior consultation where required.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds the qualifier 'where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing,' which is a reasonable narrowing/condition consistent with the regulation's assistance duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall assist' is mandatory and matches the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract covers the same subject matter and is at least as specific, extending to prior consultation of supervisory authorities where required, which is within the DPIA compliance assistance context.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation includes as one security measure the ability to restore availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident, and the contract sub-item states exactly that obligation/content.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by a physical or technical incident and require timely restoration of availability/access.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract parent clause is mandatory ('shall implement... including inter alia as appropriate') and the sub-item is part of that mandatory security-measures set.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the timing ('in a timely manner') and object ('availability and access to Personal Data') match the regulation's requirement; no weaker constraint appears.
results.contract_source_text: the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Controller Assistance
results.core_alignment_check: processor/controller assistance obligation: the contract requires the processor to assist the controller, but only for responding to data subject rights requests, not for ensuring the security of processing
results.condition_check: compatible in part, but the triggering situation is different (data subject rights requests vs security of processing)
results.modality_check: equal-or-greater force for the stated assistance duty
results.other_constraints_check: technical and organisational measures / insofar as possible are compatible, but they apply to a different underlying task
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller by appropriate technical and organisational measures, insofar as this is possible, for the fulfilment of the Controller's obligation by law to respond to requests for exercising the Data Subjects’ rights laid down in chapter III of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: Both address processor liability for damages, but the regulation is a specific GDPR liability rule limited to damage caused by processing and conditioned on non-compliance with processor-directed GDPR obligations or acting outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions. The contract instead imposes general liability for damages under Danish law subject to contractual limitations, without capturing the GDPR-specific trigger or scope.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: the regulation has specific liability conditions tied to GDPR processor obligations and controller instructions; the contract uses a different, broader general-law liability framework.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory liability statements, but the contract does not impose the same GDPR-specific mandatory liability standard.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation is limited to damage caused by processing and to the specified GDPR-related circumstances; the contract is broader and refers to general Danish law with clause-based limitations, so the substantive constraints do not match.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall be liable for damages in accordance with the general rules of Danish law subject to the limitations set out in this clause 13.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller immediately when an instruction infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions. The contract requires the processor to inform the controller before processing only when it is required by Union or Member State law to process contrary to instructions, which is a different trigger and narrower/different subject matter.
results.condition_check: Different condition: regulation is triggered by an instruction infringing GDPR/data protection law; contract is triggered by a legal requirement to process despite instructions.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory obligations, but the contract's duty is only for the separate legal-requirement scenario, not the infringement scenario.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract timing ('before Processing') is not equivalent to 'immediately' for the required trigger, and the target event differs.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the contract parent clause and sub-item address processor/controller technical and organisational security measures for processing, including a process for testing, assessing and evaluating their effectiveness, which matches the Art. 32(1)(d) security-testing obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both concern regular/ongoing assessment and evaluation of the effectiveness of security measures for processing.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses mandatory 'shall' / required measures, meeting the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract’s security-measures clause is at least as strong as the regulation and includes the relevant security-testing/evaluation process; no weaker timing or scope constraint appears.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). Appendix 3 Costs for assistance and audits This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. In the event that Controller requires assistance from the Processor or any of the Processor’s Sub-processors pursuant to this Agreement, such assistance is charged as follows:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Same actor (processor), same action (notify), same recipient (controller), same object (personal data breach awareness trigger).
results.condition_check: Compatible: notification is triggered after becoming aware of a personal data breach in both texts.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'will notify' is an обязательment equivalent in force to 'shall notify'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'without undue delay' matches exactly.
results.contract_source_text: Processor will notify the Controller without undue delay after becoming aware of a Personal Data Breach.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both address regular testing/assessing/evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational security measures for processing.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract states a process for testing/assessing/evaluating, which matches the regulation's regular testing/assessment/evaluation requirement.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses an affirmative obligation/commitment within the clause context, not a mere permission.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's wording covers the same security-measures effectiveness review; no contradictory timing, target, or location constraint appears.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). In the event that Controller requires assistance from the Processor or any of the Processor’s Sub-processors pursuant to this Agreement, such assistance is charged as follows:

shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment And Prior Consultation
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both are processor assistance duties to help the controller with prior consultation of supervisory authorities under GDPR Article 36 in high-risk processing contexts; the contract also includes DPIA assistance, which is adjacent but does not negate the Article 36 assistance.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation triggers where processing would result in high risk absent mitigation measures; the contract says 'where necessary' and 'prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required,' which is substantively aligned and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compliant: 'to assist' imposes an obligation of equal force to 'shall assist'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's reference to assisting with prior consulting of supervisory authorities under articles 35 and 36, taking into account the nature of the processing, matches the regulatory timing and subject matter; it is not looser.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires that persons acting under the processor's authority with access to personal data process them only on the controller's instructions. The contract requires the processor to process personal data only in accordance with the controller's instructions, which substantively covers the same instruction-only limitation for processing by persons under the processor's authority.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both apply to processing of personal data by persons under the processor's authority / the processor, with the same instruction-based condition; the legal-law exception and notice duty in the contract are additional and do not weaken the core rule.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses a mandatory restriction ('only in accordance with') that is at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall ensure'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract is at least as strict, and even expressly extends to transfers and adds a lawful-processing exception plus notice requirement; none of these make it looser than the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation allows processing without documented instructions only when required by Union or Member State law. The contract instead requires processing only on documented instructions from the Controller and treats the agreement itself as those instructions; it does not address a legal-law exception.
results.condition_check: Not compatible: the regulation's trigger is a requirement under Union or Member State law, while the contract's trigger is documented instructions from the Controller.
results.modality_check: The contract is stricter on instructions generally, but it does not include the legal exception required by the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching exception for processing required by law; the contract is narrower and omits that circumstance.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both provisions impose the processor's duty to ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process that data only on the controller's instructions, with the same EU/national-law exception.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract's exception for processing required under applicable EU law or national legislation matches the regulation's exception.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Must ensure' is at least as strong as 'shall ensure'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'Persons performing work for the Processor and who have access to Personal Data' is substantively equivalent to 'any natural person acting under its authority who has access to personal data'; the instruction-only constraint is the same.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor must ensure that the persons performing work for the Processor and who have access to Personal Data, only process such Personal Data as instructed by the Controller, unless processing is required under applicable EU law or national legislation. 13. Liability

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Partial overlap: both concern the content of a personal data breach notification, but the contract only covers the nature of the breach and does not include the other required items (DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures).
results.condition_check: Compatible: no conflicting triggering condition is stated.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall describe' is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's requirement to include information.
results.other_constraints_check: Looser on content scope: the contract is narrower, covering only one required element and adding 'where possible' without the missing mandatory elements.
results.contract_source_text: The aforementioned notification shall describe the nature of the Personal Data Breach including where possible:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Different obligation: the regulation requires the processor to notify the controller of a personal data breach; the contract requires the controller to notify the processor of possible unauthorized use/security breaches related to the Main Agreement.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: regulation triggers on becoming aware of a personal data breach; contract triggers on controller awareness of possible unauthorized use of credentials or other security breaches.
results.modality_check: Stronger than required on timing/force for its own duty ('shall immediately'), but it is the wrong actor and direction.
results.other_constraints_check: Different recipient and subject matter; 'in writing' and 'immediately' are stricter, but the object is not the same personal data breach notification duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller shall immediately notify the Processor in writing after becoming aware of any possible unauthorised use of log-in information, passwords, credentials or other security breaches related to the Main Agreement. 7. Records of Processing activities

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Controller Assistance
results.core_alignment_check: Processor assists Controller in fulfilling the Controller's obligation to respond to data subject rights requests; same real-world assistance duty, including chapter III rights requests.
results.condition_check: Where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing
results.modality_check: Shall assist / obligation, with 'insofar as this is possible' as a compatible qualifier and not weaker than the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: Appropriate technical and organisational measures; same target and purpose, with equivalent or stricter manner/means constraints.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller by appropriate technical and organisational measures, insofar as this is possible, for the fulfilment of the Controller's obligation by law to respond to requests for exercising the Data Subjects’ rights laid down in chapter III of the GDPR;

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Processor obligation to process personal data only in accordance with the Controller's instructions matches the regulation's requirement to process personal data on documented instructions from the controller.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on controller instructions, with the contract adding a lawful-compulsion exception and notice duty that does not weaken the core instruction requirement.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: 'shall process only in accordance with' is mandatory and restrictive, satisfying 'shall process on documented instructions'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is at least as strict, and expressly includes transfers to third countries/international organisations plus a legal requirement carve-out, which does not contradict the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: processor transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation only under controller instructions/authorization; same real-world obligation as requiring prior specific or general authorization from the controller
results.condition_check: compatible: contract allows such transfers only in accordance with controller instructions, which is at least as strict as requiring prior specific or general authorization; the legal-compulsion carve-out is an additional permitted exception
results.modality_check: not weaker: 'only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller' is restrictive/mandatory and satisfies a 'shall not ... without authorization' requirement
results.other_constraints_check: same target and scope: transfers of personal data to a third country or international organisation; timing is effectively prior authorization via instructions, with no looser timing

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational security measures appropriate to risk; the contract clause likewise requires the Controller and the Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to risk, including examples such as encryption and confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are conditioned on taking into account the state of the art/costs/nature, scope, context and purposes of processing and the risk to rights and freedoms of natural persons.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses 'shall implement', which is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract includes equivalent security-related constraints and examples; it is not narrower than the regulation and is at least coextensive.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to return or delete all personal data to the controller after services end. The contract requires the processor, upon expiry, to delete all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data, and also gives the Controller access to export/return data during the agreement. The deletion obligation directly matches the regulatory duty; the export/access language is additional and does not weaken it.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation triggers after the end of the provision of services relating to processing; the contract triggers upon expiry of the Agreement, which is substantively equivalent. The storage carve-out for EU or Member State law is a permissible legal exception and does not make the duty weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible. "Will delete" is mandatory and at least as strong as "shall return or delete."
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The object is all personal data / all of Controller’s data including Personal Data, which covers the required scope. The timing is equivalent, and the optional return/export access during the agreement is additional rather than contradictory.
results.contract_source_text: Processor will give Controller access to system functionality in order for the Controller to delete and/or return (i.e. export) any and all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data during the Agreement. Upon expiry of the Agreement the Processor will delete all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data, unless EU or national Member State law requires storage of the Personal Data.

results.contract_source_text: In the event that Processor engages Sub-processors for carrying out Processing activities on behalf of the Controller in accordance with Appendix 5, the same data protection obligations as set out in this Agreement shall be imposed on that Sub-processor by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of this Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both clauses impose the processor's duty to process personal data only on the controller's instructions/documented instructions, including the same processor-to-controller instruction relationship.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are triggered on request / on demand availability; the contract's 'on request' matches the regulatory availability duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall make available' is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's obligation.

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall make the records available to the Controller and the supervisory authority on request. 8. Costs for assistance and audits

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor/controller to implement appropriate technical and organisational security measures, including encryption/pseudonymisation, confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience, timely restoration of availability/access, and regular testing/evaluation. The contract parent clause and sub-item expressly require the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, and specifically includes encryption of personal data and the ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services. This directly overlaps with the Art. 32 security obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are conditioned on taking into account the state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes of processing, and risk to rights and freedoms. The contract uses substantially the same risk-based qualifier.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation imposes a mandatory duty; the contract uses 'shall implement', which is equally mandatory.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's 'appropriate technical and organisational measures' and the listed security measures are at least as strict as the regulation's examples. It does not contradict the requirement, and the included encryption/confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience measures align with the security objective.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both provisions address when the processor is liable for damage/loss in connection with processing. The regulation limits processor liability to cases where the processor itself failed processor-directed GDPR obligations or acted contrary to lawful controller instructions. The contract instead allocates non-liability to the processor for damage caused by the controller’s own failures, controller breaches of other agreements, or controller failures toward supervisory authorities. This is related to liability for processing damage, but it does not state the same processor-liability trigger or limitation.
results.condition_check: Not aligned. The regulation’s conditions are processor non-compliance with processor-specific GDPR duties or acting outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions. The contract conditions are controller failures/breaches and supervisory-authority penalties arising from the controller’s conduct.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a non-liability clause, which is not weaker in force than the regulation’s liability limitation, but it applies to different triggering circumstances.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching substantive constraints on the same liability trigger; the contract adds different causal scenarios and parties rather than the regulation’s processor-specific unlawful processing conditions.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall not be liable for loss or damages if caused by the Controller’s failure to comply with its obligations according to applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulations or this Agreement. results.contract_source_text: In the event that Processor engages Sub-processors for carrying out Processing activities on behalf of the Controller in accordance with Appendix 5, the same data protection obligations as set out in this Agreement shall be imposed on that Sub-processor by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of this Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation gives the controller a right to terminate the DPA in certain cases. The contract gives each party, including the controller, an entitlement to terminate the agreement on 60 days' written notice if the parties cannot agree in good faith on reasonable terms for implementing changes needed for GDPR or other applicable data protection law compliance. This is the same real-world termination right, and it is not narrower in substance.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by specified cases involving compliance-related changes / circumstances where agreement cannot be reached.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract grants an entitlement to terminate, which meets a right to terminate.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract adds a 60-day written notice and automatic termination of related agreements, which are additional constraints but not weaker than the regulation's requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires organizational and technical security measures for processing personal data, including confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience and related security safeguards. The contract obligates the Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures so that processing meets GDPR requirements and ensures protection of data subjects' rights. This is the same real-world security/compliance obligation, framed more generally and at least as strictly.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation is a general security obligation under Art. 32(1); the contract applies generally to processing of personal data and applicable data protection law, with no weaker triggering condition.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses "agrees to implement" and "will meet" / "ensure," which are mandatory commitments, not mere permission.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract is broader and more general than the regulation, but not contradictory or looser on timing, manner, target, or location.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires that, under a general written authorization, the controller be informed of intended changes and have the right to object to additions or replacements of sub-processors. results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered by intended changes to the addition or replacement of sub-processors after the controller has been informed. The contract addresses initial general authorization and an existing list, not the change-notice/object mechanism.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a controller right to object; the contract grants authorization to the processor to use sub-processors, which is a different and weaker/different obligation and does not include the objection right.
results.other_constraints_check: The specific target is changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors. The contract only names one current sub-processor (Microsoft Azure) and a general authorization, with no equivalent change-control or objection timing.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires technical and organisational security measures, including encryption, confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience of processing systems and services, and appropriate security measures overall. The contract’s measures are at least as strict as the regulation’s examples: encryption is included, and the confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience requirement is matched. The contract does not contradict the timely restoration or testing/evaluation aspects, but it does not need to enumerate every example if it covers the same security-measures obligation overall.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires appropriate technical and organisational security measures, including encryption/pseudonymisation, confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience, timely restoration of availability/access, and regular testing/evaluation. The contract parent clause obligates the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, and the sub-item expressly includes encryption of personal data and the ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services. This directly overlaps with Art. 32(1)(a) and (b), and the general security-measures obligation covers the same security duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are conditioned on taking into account the state of the art, implementation costs, nature/scope/context/purposes of processing, and risk to rights and freedoms; the contract is substantively aligned and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses a mandatory "shall implement" obligation, which meets the regulation's required security-measures duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible for the overlapping parts. The contract's encryption is narrower in example form (transmission/remote access) but still an encryption measure; the confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience language matches the regulation. results.core_alignment_check: The regulation prohibits the processor from transferring personal data to a third country or international organization unless the controller has given prior specific or general authorization. results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller immediately if an instruction infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions. results.condition_check: No matching condition: the regulation is triggered by an instruction that infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions; the contract contains no such notification trigger.

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the controller to have a right to terminate the DPA in certain cases. The contract gives each party, including the controller, an entitlement to terminate the agreement on 60 days' written notice if the parties cannot agree in good faith on reasonable terms for implementing changes necessary for GDPR or other applicable data protection law compliance. This is the same real-world termination right, and it is not narrower than the regulation.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the triggering condition is a failure to agree in good faith on reasonable implementation terms for legally necessary changes, which fits within the regulation's 'in certain cases' formulation.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall be entitled to terminate' grants a right, which meets the required right to terminate.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract adds a 60-day written notice requirement and automatic termination of related agreements, which are additional constraints but not looser than the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations; the contract parent clause requires the processor to maintain records of all categories of processing activities, and the sub-item names one required record item ('the categories of Processing carried out on behalf of the Controller'). results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires appropriate technical and organisational security measures, including encryption/pseudonymisation, confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience, timely restoration, and regular testing/evaluation; the contract requires the controller and processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including encryption of personal data in transit/remote access and ensuring ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on risk/state of the art/nature-scope-context-purposes; the contract is at least as specific and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall implement' and thus imposes a mandatory obligation equal to or stronger than the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's measures are framed as appropriate to the risk and include encryption and ongoing confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience, which substantively matches the security-measures requirement; it does not contradict the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

The contract instead obliges the processor to assist the controller with carrying out a DPIA and prior consultation; it does not itself state or include the DPIA content.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract's 'where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing' qualifier is a reasonable limitation on the assistance duty and does not make it weaker than the regulation's content requirement.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory assistance obligation ('to assist'), which is not weaker than the regulation's 'shall at least include' for the assistance duty, but it is a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting timing, manner, or location constraints; however, the clause concerns assistance with DPIA/consultation rather than the DPIA's required contents.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the DPA to contain the categories of data subjects (a content item about persons affected), while the contract sub-item provides a general description of technical and organisational security measures, which is a different content axis and not data-subject categories.
results.condition_check: No special condition in the regulation; the contract item is just a security-measures description and does not match the required content.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory, but modality is irrelevant because the subject matter does not align.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract points to Appendix 2 for security measures and concerns security content, not the required categories of data subjects; this is an axis mismatch and a pointer to different content.
results.contract_source_text: a general description of the technical and organisational security measures as referred to in Appendix 2 and subsequent amendments thereto.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the DPA itself to contain the types of personal data processed; the contract clause only authorizes processing and gives processing instructions, but does not state any types of personal data.
results.condition_check: No specific condition issue; the requirement is unconditional.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory processing terms, but it does not provide the required content.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/manner/location content for the required item is supplied; the clause is a pointer to processing terms, not a statement of personal data types.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to ensure that persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process them only on the controller's instructions. The contract instead addresses legislative changes and possible changes to instructions, with termination if the parties cannot agree; it does not impose the specific instruction-only processing duty on authorized persons.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / no matching condition for the specific duty; the clause is triggered by legislative changes and agreement on changed instructions, not by access to personal data by persons under the processor's authority.
results.modality_check: The contract uses obligations about compliance with legislative changes and a termination right, but it does not contain the required mandatory instruction-only processing obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing/termination mechanics and GDPR-change context are unrelated to the required manner of processing under controller instructions.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to ensure that natural persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process it only on the controller's instructions. results.condition_check: The regulation's condition is access to personal data by natural persons acting under the processor's authority. The contract's condition is legislative changes / changes to instructions and inability to agree terms, which is unrelated.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a general reasonable-endeavours/termination framework, not an obligation to ensure instruction-only processing by authorized persons.
results.other_constraints_check: The required constraint is that processing occurs only on controller instructions; the contract addresses compliance with legal changes and agreement termination timing, not that manner of processing.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. results.core_alignment_check: The regulation gives the controller a right to suspend processing in certain cases. results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory about notice and objection mechanics, but it does not impose or preserve the controller's right to suspend processing.
results.other_constraints_check: The subject matter, trigger, and remedy differ: suspension of processing versus objection to sub-processor changes and deemed acceptance.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the content of a personal data breach notification to include specific information (nature of breach, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures). The contract instead requires the Controller to notify the Processor of possible unauthorized use of credentials or other security breaches; it is an incident-notification duty to a different recipient, not a clause stating the required contents of an Art. 33(3) breach notice.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: the regulation is triggered by a personal data breach notification, while the contract is triggered by becoming aware of possible unauthorized use of log-in information/passwords/credentials or other security breaches related to the Main Agreement.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'shall immediately notify' obligation, which is not weaker than the regulation's 'shall at least include' wording, but it is about a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and manner differ but are not the main issue; the contract requires immediate written notice, which is stricter on timing/form. However, it does not supply the required notification contents.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires that a personal data breach notification include the consequences of the breach and the measures taken or proposed to mitigate its effects. The contract sub-item, read with the parent clause, requires the notification to describe the nature of the breach and include reasonable measures taken or proposed by the Processor to mitigate the consequences of the Personal Data Breach. This matches the mitigation-measures component and is substantively aligned, though it does not cover every listed element of Article 33(3).
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both apply in the context of a personal data breach notification; the contract does not add a conflicting condition.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The parent clause uses 'shall describe' and the sub-item states the content to be included, which is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'Reasonable measures taken or proposed' is not weaker than the regulation's 'measures taken or proposed to mitigate its effects'; it is substantively the same mitigation obligation.
results.contract_source_text: reasonable measures taken or proposed by the Processor to mitigate the consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in ensuring security of processing. The contract text mainly describes security measures, risk assessment, hosting location, and where security policies are described, plus a fee appendix for assistance if the controller requires it. It does not itself impose a substantive duty on the processor to assist the controller with ensuring security; it only references assistance in a charging context and points to external policies.
results.condition_check: The regulation is unconditional assistance in ensuring security. The contract's assistance reference is conditional on the controller requiring assistance and is framed as costs for assistance, not a matching substantive duty.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a shall-duty. The contract does not clearly impose an equivalent processor obligation to assist; the relevant text is not an obligation of equal force but a fee provision tied to requested assistance.
results.other_constraints_check: The subject matter overlaps with security of processing, but the contract's main substance is about security measures, hosting, and external policy references rather than the assistance obligation itself.

results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.
